strengths: Exceptionally high aggregate Win Rate of 92.8% over a very large sample of 24,902 trades, confirming a powerful predictive signal.
The signal's predictive power shows minimal decay, dropping only from 93.7% (parent spec) to 92.8% when the prediction horizon was extended from 1 to 5 ticks.
Performance is highly consistent across symbols and days, with all individual backtest WRs staying within a tight 3.4 percentage point range (91.3% to 94.6%).
The strategy is profitable with a strong aggregate expectancy of +6.34 bps per trade.
weaknesses: The sustained WR above 90% is unusually high for financial markets, suggesting a potential risk of overfitting or an idealized backtest environment that may not fully capture real-world trading frictions like latency and fees.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The spec confirms a remarkably strong and persistent edge, with WR=92.8% holding strong even at a 5-tick horizon. The next logical step is to tighten the threshold (e.g., from 0.5 to 0.6 or 0.7) to test if this dose-dependent signal can yield an even higher-conviction subset of trades, further validating its robustness.
